name: Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular desde Molinos de Duero
description: Cycle a moderate 23.0-mile (37.1 km) loop around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo from Molinos de Duero, enjoying reservoir views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Molinos De Duero traverse a landscape shaped by the Picos de Urbión, the Duero River, and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. The region features extensive pine and oak forests, offering varied terrain from riverside paths to mountain ascents. Cyclists can expect routes that combine natural beauty with historical elements, including remnants of Roman roads. Best touring cycling routes around Molinos De Duero The most popular touring cycling route is Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop, a 23.0 miles (37.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and passes through surrounding pine forests. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna Negra Ascent. – Molinos de Duero loop from Vinuesa, a difficult 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain towards the emblematic glacial lake in the Urbión mountain range. Local touring cyclists also love the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Vinuesa, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through pine and oak forests around the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Molinos De Duero is defined by the Picos de Urbión, the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Molinos De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 from over 13 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Molinos De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Molinos De Duero?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Molinos De Duero, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 13 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Molinos De Duero?
A: The terrain around Molinos De Duero is quite varied, shaped by the Picos de Urbión, the Duero River, and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. You'll find extensive pine and oak forests, offering shaded routes and a distinct natural ambiance. Routes can range from riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents, with a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. For instance, the route to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24313894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt; involves significant elevation gain, while reservoir loops offer more moderate terrain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Molinos De Duero?
A: The region is generally excellent for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for touring cycling. Summers can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but the extensive pine forests provide shade. Winter conditions, particularly in the higher elevations of the Picos de Urbión, can be challenging due to snow and colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Molinos De Duero?
A: Yes, there are a few easier routes available. While many routes are moderate to difficult, you can find options that are less demanding. For example, some sections along the Duero River or shorter loops around the reservoir might be more suitable for beginners. Always check the route's difficulty grade on komoot before heading out.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Molinos De Duero?
A: The touring cycling routes in Molinos De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 out of 5 stars. Over 500 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the stunning natural beauty, the diverse terrain, and the peaceful atmosphere of the pine forests and reservoir views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Molinos De Duero are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041722151/cuerda-del-pozo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24313894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra Ascent. – Molinos de Duero loop from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and difficulties.

Q: Can I cycle around the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir?
A: Absolutely! The Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir is a central feature for touring cyclists in the area. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041722151/cuerda-del-pozo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; (37.1 km) offer beautiful views of the water and pass through surrounding pine forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 20.3 km trail.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Molinos De Duero offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24313894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra Ascent. – Molinos de Duero loop from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.4 km path with significant elevation gain towards the emblematic glacial lake. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Restaurante Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 55.3 km with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle towards the dramatic Picos de Urbión, visit the stunning Laguna Negra, or enjoy views of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. The extensive pine forests themselves are a significant natural feature. Additionally, you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5578148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Piedra Andadera&lt;/a&gt; natural monument.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike in the area?
A: While some waterfalls might require a short walk from a cycling path, the region around Molinos De Duero is known for several beautiful waterfalls. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso Peñoncito Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Planning your route to include these natural attractions can enhance your cycling experience.

Q: Can I find family-friendly touring cycling routes in Molinos De Duero?
A: Yes, some routes are suitable for families, particularly those with less elevation gain and on well-maintained surfaces. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, shorter, flatter sections along the Duero River or around the reservoir can be good options. Always check the route's distance and difficulty to ensure it matches your family's abilities.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions to visit while cycling?
A: Yes, the area blends natural beauty with historical elements. You can find remnants of a Roman road that once linked ancient towns. Molinos de Duero itself boasts traditional Soria architecture, including the Gothic Parish Church of San Martín de Tours. The nearby town of Abejar also hosts a Museum of Cycling, which could be an interesting stop for enthusiasts.

Q: Is there public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: Molinos de Duero is a charming town, but public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the area or specific starting points for routes. Many cyclists prefer to drive to Molinos de Duero and start their tours from there, utilizing available parking.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Tour around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular from Molinos de Duero typically takes about 2 hours and 37 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This estimate is for the full 37.1-kilometer loop with approximately 385 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While categorized as 'moderate' and requiring good fitness, the route is generally considered suitable for all skill levels. Its primarily paved surface makes it accessible, but the distance and elevation gain mean a basic level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. Most sections are well-paved, though you might encounter some areas with looser terrain.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir and surrounding pine forests. Notable highlights include the charming village of Molinos de Duero, the area around Vinuesa and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, and the popular beaches Playa Gamella and Playa Pita.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Soria region offers diverse landscapes, and cycling is enjoyable across many seasons. Consider spring and autumn for milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so plan for early morning or late afternoon rides.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with several official trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Ruta del Duero (27%), Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: Soria-Abejar (25%), Ruta del Sureste (25%), Etapa 02: Vinuesa – Soria (18%), and Etapa 01: Fuentes del Duero – Vinuesa (7%).

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information specifically regarding dogs on this exact cycling route is not available. However, generally, dogs are often allowed on trails in natural areas, but it's always best to keep them on a leash for their safety and to respect wildlife and other users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Tour around the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — circular from Molinos de Duero. It's a public route through natural areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Molinos de Duero, you can typically find parking within the village. Look for designated parking areas or suitable street parking, respecting local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find options for food and drink in Molinos de Duero at the start and end of your tour. Additionally, the route passes through or near other settlements like Vinuesa, which may offer cafes and restaurants.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, essential items include sufficient water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and a helmet. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Vinuesa and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir
